message:
chore(beads): tick 381 — file ft-l9mxa for mid-flight cancel gap

Filed ft-l9mxa (P3) documenting the tick-380 finding:
DistributedHttpClient's response-read path does not observe
mid-flight cx-cancel.

Impact: lower-severity than ft-kfkyi. Not a security issue — hung
distributed RPC calls will eventually time out via internal limits
but won't observe operator-initiated cancel. An agent workflow
firing cx.cancel() to abort a slow peer call will wait the full
internal-timeout window before the call returns.

Fix approach options recorded in the bead description:
1. Race inner future against cx-cancel future (narrowest).
2. Patch asupersync::http::h1 to thread cx through await points
   (bigger, upstream).
3. Wrap in timeout_with_cx — doesn't help since tick 328 showed
   that primitive only observes budget, not cancel.

Existing tick-380 snapshot test
(`distributed_http_client_mid_flight_cancel_does_not_hang`) is the
flip trigger: when the fix lands, elapsed drops from ~10s to
<< 10s and the Err shape changes. Closer updates the assertion
to require the faster behavior.

Linked as `related` to ft-xbnl0.2.4. Not blocking closure.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
